rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halogen (Br2/Cl2) shuttle transferring vicinal dihalides from a saturated donor (e.g., vicinal dibromide or polychloroalkane) to an alkene acceptor to furnish a new vicinal dihalide and an alkene byproduct via a paired-electrolysis e-Shuttle process.
electrochemical_role: Paired electrolysis: cathode reduces the vicinal dihalide donor (2 e-) releasing halide and an alkene; halide is oxidized at the anode (directly for Br-, or via Mn2+/Mn3+ mediator for Cl-) to generate an electrophilic halonium/X+ that adds across the acceptor alkene to give the vicinal dihalide product.

key_intermediates.name: halide anion (Br- or Cl-) released by cathodic cleavage of donor C-X bond
alkene byproduct from reductive dehalogenation of donor
bromonium / Br+ electrophile generated at anode
Mn(III)-Cl oxidant generated by anodic oxidation (Cl2 cycle)
vicinal dihalide product from X+ addition to acceptor alkene

electron_flow: At the cathode, two electrons reduce the vicinal dihalide donor, cleaving both C-X bonds to give an alkene and 2 X-. The X- migrates to the anode where it is oxidized (directly to Br+ for bromides, or via a Mn2+/Mn3+ redox mediator for chlorides) and the resulting electrophilic halogen adds across the acceptor alkene to deliver the vicinal dihalide product.

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dibromoethane (donor probe for HX elimination)
probe_reagents.observation: Used as Br2 donor; absence of HBr-elimination products supports a 2e- dehalogenation pathway rather than E2/HX elimination.

probe_reagents.name: 2,3-dibromobutane
probe_reagents.observation: Donor probe; ethylene/butene byproduct profile consistent with reductive vicinal dehalogenation, ruling out HX elimination.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1,1,2-tetrachloroethane
probe_reagents.observation: Cl2 donor; product distribution rules out HCl elimination pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,2-dichloroethane (DCE)
probe_reagents.observation: Cl2 donor; consistent with reductive dechlorination releasing ethylene + 2 Cl-.

probe_reagents.name: Lindane (γ-HCH)
probe_reagents.observation: Cl2 donor; formation of benzene (confirmed by crude NMR and GC) supports stepwise reductive dechlorination rather than HCl elimination.

notes: Authors explicitly designed Section 6 experiments to exclude an HX-elimination pathway from the donor; the favored mechanism is 2e- cathodic vicinal dehalogenation + anodic halide oxidation (direct for Br, Mn-mediated for Cl). A representative intramolecular Br2 transfer (Section 8) and CV studies (Section 7) further support discrete halide/halonium intermediates. Mn2+/Mn3+ is essential as an anodic mediator for the Cl2 cycle because direct Cl- oxidation is too anodic.
